name: Cod Cobbler (Irish)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place cod fillets in the bottom of a round oven dish.
2. Make a cheese sauce with 2 oz each of butter and flour, 1/2 l milk, and 3 1/2 oz grated cheese. Pour over the fish.
3. Make scone dough: rub 2 oz butter into 8 oz flour with 1 tsp baking powder and pinch of salt. Add 2 oz grated cheese (preferably mature Cheddar or a mixture of that and Parmesan). Drop 1 egg yolk into the mixture and add enough milk to make a workable dough.
4. Roll out to a thickness of 1/2 inch and cut into small rounds with a scone cutter. Arrange the rounds on top of the sauce, so that they just about cover the surface.
5. Glaze the scones with a little milk and sprinkle some more grated cheese over them.
6. Bake in a hot oven (450°F) for 25-30 minutes, until the scones are golden brown.
